analysis.core_story_summary: Karma Vipak means the fruits one reaps according to his or her Karma. While continuing with the description of various aspects of hell, Nachiketa told the assembled sages--At yamapuri, Chitragupta goes through the record of all the deeds, committed by a man when he was alive and brings into the notice of Yamaraj. Yamaraj then decides upon the punishment to be given to the concerned soul.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Contains conflict imagery that may need age-appropriate framing.

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **Karma Vipak — Kids (8-12):** Karma Vipak means the fruits one reaps according to his or her Karma. While continuing with the description of various aspects of hell, Nachiketa told the assembled sages--At yamapuri, Chitragupta goes through the record of all the deeds, committed by a man when he was alive and brings into the notice of Yamaraj. Yamaraj then decides upon the punishment to be given to the concerned soul. This story teaches the cost of conflict. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.

redesigned_story_by_demographic.devotional_traditional: **Karma Vipak — Devotional/Traditional Readers:** Karma Vipak means the fruits one reaps according to his or her Karma. While continuing with the description of various aspects of hell, Nachiketa told the assembled sages--At yamapuri, Chitragupta goes through the record of all the deeds, committed by a man when he was alive and brings into the notice of Yamaraj. Yamaraj then decides upon the punishment to be given to the concerned soul. The episode highlights the cost of conflict. redesigned_story_by_demographic.global_general: **Karma Vipak — Global General Audience:** Karma Vipak means the fruits one reaps according to his or her Karma. While continuing with the description of various aspects of hell, Nachiketa told the assembled sages--At yamapuri, Chitragupta goes through the record of all the deeds, committed by a man when he was alive and brings into the notice of Yamaraj. Yamaraj then decides upon the punishment to be given to the concerned soul. In universal terms, it explores the cost of conflict.
